Tommaso Grimaldi, inquisitor of the Venetian state, returns to the burned-out
Zecca the morning after the fire. He has been given three weeks to close the
case. What he finds in the ashes contradicts what the masters of the mint
told the Council, and the only witness willing to speak is an apothecary who
will not cross the bridge at Ponte San Giovanni by daylight.

Three chapters. Tommaso narrates in close third. The story ends with the
inquiry widening, not closing.
